hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 35th percentile; overheal (72.0%) exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse (59.5%) by 12.5 points, and HPM (3.08) ran at just 0.53x the sample average - the clearest story of this kill. Active time (99.4%) was in line with the sample.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (14.9%) undershot the sample's 27.6% share, the largest gap this kill; Rejuvenation (25.2%) and Lifebloom (51.1%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (14.4% and 41.0%) to compensate.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 85% sample usage rate - the only real cooldown deviation this kill. Swiftmend was cast once (on Victim) against a sample average of 3.8 casts, a real if not individually flagged undercast. No deaths this kill.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (90.1%) ran above the sample average (83.5%), though the top target (Thugalina, 39.7%) took a smaller share than the sample's typical top-target concentration (48.5%) - wide coverage without a single dominant recipient.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 69th percentile, the best relative kill of the raid; overheal (56.2%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (49.1%) by 7.1 points, the narrowest gap so far. HPM (4.96) ran at 0.67x the sample average, the closest to sample-normal of any kill this far. Active time (99.3%) was in line.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (23.5%) undershot the sample's 39.2% share; Rejuvenation (28.0%) and Swiftmend (17.2%) both ran well ahead of their sample shares (14.0% and 4.7%) to compensate.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Two real deviations this kill: Innervate went unused against a 90% sample usage rate, and Nature's Swiftness also went unused against a 75% rate. Swiftmend was cast 7 times (self, Captinhelp x2, Banepain, Beublic), above the sample's own average of 3.2. No deaths this kill.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (72.2%) ran above the sample average (56.8%); top-target concentration (31.7%, Captinhelp) landed in line with the sample's typical 25.2%.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 23rd percentile; overheal (62.1%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (54.3%) by 7.8 points, and HPM (3.81) ran at 0.6x the sample average. By far the deadliest kill of the raid at 22 deaths - more than double the next-highest kill (Solarian, 9) - including Vinnyvozz's own death twice.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (12.3%) undershot the sample's 52.8% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; Rejuvenation (40.4%) ran far ahead of the sample's 13.3% to compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (both self), in line with the sample. Rebirth went unused against a real 26% sample usage rate on this specific boss - a modest rate, but worth naming given 22 real deaths on the board. Checking every death against the nearest cooldown cast, none lines up as a clean missed save: the closest gaps run 1.1-13.2 seconds past a Swiftmend or Nature's Swiftness cast aimed at other targets, and the later deaths trail a single early Mana Potion cast (which doesn't heal) by 7-52 seconds. This reads as a real, sustained damage event the healing couldn't keep pace with, not a series of individually missed cooldowns.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (52.7%) and top-target concentration (13.7%, Megatauro) both landed in line with the sample (51.4% and 15.7%).

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 21st percentile, the weakest kill of the raid so far; overheal (58.2%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.5%) by 11.7 points, and HPM (4.62) ran at 0.56x the sample average. Active time (99.6%) was in line, and 2 deaths this kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (17.7%) undershot the sample's 33.7% share, the largest gap this kill; Swiftmend (15.9%) and Rejuvenation (22.5%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (4.3% and 12.7%) to compensate.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed this pipeline's deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self), and Swiftmend was cast 8 times across 8 targets, above the sample's own average of 2.8. Of the 2 deaths, Ghostpepperx's trails a Nature's Swiftness cast by 2.1 seconds (not a real correlation, since that ability doesn't heal), but Megatauro's death follows a Swiftmend cast by just 1.1 seconds - and Megatauro is one of that cast's real targets, so this reads as an attempted save that was overwhelmed by damage, not a missed cooldown.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Both target coverage (52.0% vs. a 74.9% sample average) and top-target concentration (12.1%, Banepain, vs. a 34.9% sample average) ran well below the sample - the opposite pattern from Lurker's wider-than-usual spread, and a real, notable deviation this kill.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 47th percentile, the best relative kill of the raid so far; overheal (66.0%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (51.1%) by 14.9 points, and HPM (4.12) ran at 0.58x the sample average - the same pattern as every other kill, just at a stronger percentile. Tree of Life was inactive this kill (0%), same as Lurker.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation (24.3%) ran well ahead of the sample's 13.0% share, the largest gap this kill; Lifebloom (29.2%) undershot the sample's 37.4%. Tranquility shows 0 casts in the cooldown table below, but the real healing data shows it was genuinely cast once (6.5% of healing, guid 44208) - this class's Tranquility guid isn't wired into the cooldown-tracking table yet, so the table undercounts it rather than showing a real absence.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 87% sample usage rate - the only real cooldown deviation. Swiftmend was cast 4 times (Braila, Lasmur x2, Enëlye), under the sample's own average of 5.1. Of the 2 deaths (both Lasmur), the first trails an Innervate cast by 4.2 seconds (no real correlation, since Innervate restores mana rather than healing), and the second happens 3.5 seconds before the nearest Swiftmend cast even landed - that cast came too late to have been a save attempt.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (57.5%) ran close to the sample average (67.4%); the top target (Captinhelp, 23.6%) took a notably smaller share than the sample's typical top-target concentration (38.8%) - healing was spread wider than usual at the top end.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 29th percentile; overheal (67.4%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (53.5%) by 13.9 points, and HPM (3.71) ran at 0.53x the sample average. Active time (99.9%) was in line.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (2.6%) was barely cast at all this kill, against a 21.9% sample share - the largest gap this kill; Rejuvenation (24.4%) and Swiftmend (13.5%) both ran well ahead of their sample shares (11.6% and 4.4%) to compensate.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed the deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self). Swiftmend was cast 9 times across 8 different targets, the heaviest use of the raid so far, close to the sample's own average of 6.9. Rebirth was cast once, targeting Vajomee, who had died just 4.5 seconds earlier - a real, close-timed answer to that death. The other death this kill (Ghostpepperx) trails a Swiftmend cast by 2.6 seconds, but that cast's real targets don't include Ghostpepperx, so it reads as coincidental timing rather than a missed save.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Both target coverage (38.2% vs. a 50.9% sample average) and top-target concentration (9.1%, Luffydonoo, vs. a 21.0% sample average) ran below the sample - a real, wide-spread kill with no single dominant recipient.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 35th percentile; overheal (70.8%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (61.1%) by 9.7 points, and HPM (3.37) ran at 0.54x the sample average. Active time (99.9%) was in line, and 1 death this kill.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom (38.3%) undershot the sample's 49.4% share, the largest gap this kill; Swiftmend (13.1%) and Rejuvenation (18.0%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (6.1% and 12.6%) to compensate.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed the deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self). Swiftmend was cast 6 times across 6 targets (Dormammoo, Thugalina, Captinhelp, Megatauro x2, self), close to the sample average. The one death this kill (Captinhelp) trails the nearest Swiftmend cast - which actually targeted Megatauro, not Captinhelp - by 10.7 seconds, too far and the wrong target to read as a missed save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (83.5%) ran in line with the sample average (77.9%); top-target concentration (28.2%, Captinhelp) ran below the sample's typical 42.7% - wide coverage without a single dominant recipient.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 73rd percentile, by far the best kill of the raid; overheal (41.3%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (33.0%) by 8.3 points, even at this strong percentile - continuing the raid-wide pattern. HPM (7.14) ran at 0.85x the sample average, the closest to sample-normal of any kill this raid. Active time (98.6%) was in line, and no deaths this kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (33.5%) undershot the sample's 76.6% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; both Lifebloom ranks ran far ahead of their sample shares instead (HoT rank 33.8% vs. 4.9%, Bloom rank 18.1% vs. 2.6%) - close to an inverted version of the sample's usual Regrowth-heavy composition.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against the lowest sample-usage rate of any kill this raid (55%) but still crossed this pipeline's undercast threshold. Everything else - Innervate, Swiftmend, Mana Potion - landed in line with the sample. No deaths this kill.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (68.2%) ran well above the sample average (37.2%) - the widest real coverage gap of the raid; top-target concentration (17.2%, Zitish) landed in line with the sample.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: 18th percentile, the second-weakest kill of the raid; overheal (81.6%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (65.4%) by 16.2 points, and HPM (2.23) ran at just 0.46x the sample average, among the lowest of the raid. Active time (85.8%) ran notably below the sample average (97.5%) - the only kill this raid where active time itself reads as a real deviation. Food was not active this kill, the one exception in an otherwise consumable-complete raid.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth was barely cast this kill (9.2%) against a 59.4% sample share - the single widest composition gap of the raid. Lifebloom (55.1%) and Swiftmend (26.1%) both ran far ahead of their sample shares (13.1% and 6.7%) to compensate.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against a 72% sample usage rate - the only cooldown that crossed this pipeline's deviation threshold. Rebirth also never got cast, against a real (if modest) 12% sample usage rate on this boss - still worth naming given 9 real deaths on the board, the second-most of the raid. Swiftmend was cast only 3 times (Hezrun, Bronimo, Dormammoo), well under the sample's typical volume for this kill. Most of the 9 deaths cluster tightly together within about 12 seconds of each other, all trailing that same single early Swiftmend cast by 4-16 seconds - this reads as a real, concentrated burst of damage the healing couldn't keep pace with, not a series of individually missed cooldowns.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (58.2%) and top-target concentration (15.7%, Zitish) both landed in line with the sample - the low HPS, HPM, and active-time numbers above are the real story on this kill, not target spread.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: 23rd percentile, tied with Leotheras for the raid's weaker end; overheal (68.3%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (61.7%) by 6.6 points - the narrowest gap of the raid, though still a real one. HPM (3.58) ran at 0.59x the sample average. 4 deaths this kill, including Vinnyvozz's own.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (19.4%) undershot the sample's 33.8% share; Rejuvenation (18.1%) and Swiftmend (12.5%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (10.1% and 4.9%) to compensate.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 87% sample usage rate - the only cooldown that crossed the deviation threshold. Rebirth was cast once, targeting Beublic, in line with a real 61% sample usage rate on this specific boss. Swiftmend was cast 10 times across 10 different targets, the heaviest use of the raid, close to the sample's own average. None of the 4 deaths lines up as a clean missed save: Vinnyvozz's own death trails the nearest Innervate cast by 34.8 seconds (and Innervate doesn't heal regardless), Vajomee's and Beublic's deaths trail a Swiftmend cast aimed elsewhere by 11-17 seconds, and Calamety's death trails a self-only Mana Potion cast by 34.9 seconds.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (52.3%) and top-target concentration (19.7%, Thugalina) both landed in line with the sample (57.7% and 20.9%).

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: mostly consistent - one real missing-enchant gap (Back) and a single Main Hand variant that reads as a likely benign single-kill weapon swap rather than a real inconsistency
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Back (item 29989) carries no permanent enchant across all 10 kills - the same real gap also present in Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid night (report rQpVkMjGnc4t9CqW). Main Hand differs on one kill, but the gear diff itself flags it as a likely benign single/minority-kill variant rather than a real equipping gap. Consumables (battle and guardian elixir) were complete on all 10 kills; food was the one exception, inactive on Solarian specifically. Flask was never active on any of the 10 kills, though this pipeline's own consumable-completeness check doesn't require a flask to count a kill as complete.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: The dominant story of this raid, same as Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid night, is overheal: it exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse on all 10 kills, ranging from 6.6 points past the worst (Kael'thas) to 16.2 points past it (Solarian), paired with HPM running 0.45x-0.85x the sample average on every kill. Nature's Swiftness went unused on 6 of the 10 kills (Hydross, Lurker, Morogrim, Void Reaver, Solarian, Kael'thas) against real sample usage rates of 55-87% on those specific bosses - the most consistent single cooldown gap of the raid. The three weakest kills by percentile were Solarian (18th), Karathress (21st), and Leotheras (23rd, tied with Kael'thas). Leotheras was by far the deadliest kill of the raid at 22 deaths - more than double the next-highest (Solarian, 9) - and Vinnyvozz died personally on 3 of the 10 kills (Leotheras twice, plus once each on Solarian and Kael'thas). One real gear note: the Back slot carries no permanent enchant.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse on every single kill this raid (6.6 to 16.2 points past the sample's own worst), paired with HPM running 0.45x-0.85x the sample average on all 10 kills - the same raid-wide pattern already seen in Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid night.
